channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: FL spent $660M on healthcare for illegals as hospitals ordered to verify legal status, report shows
item.description: The Florida Agency for Health Care Administration announced the latest update to its Hospital Patient Immigration Status Dashboard, which showed that the state was paying nearly $660 million to cover the health care costs of illegal immigrants last year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Longtime Democratic Sen. Jeanne Shaheen not seeking re-election in 2026 in key northeastern swing state
item.description: Democratic Sen. Jeanne Shaheen of New Hampshire is the latest Democrat in the Senate to announce her retirement rather than seek re-election in the 2026 midterms.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: In a blow to Democrats, Sen. Jeanne Shaheen will not seek reelection in New Hampshire
item.description: Democrats say no Republican has won a Senate race in New Hampshire in more than a decade and the trend will continue next year despite Democratic Sen. Jeanne Shaheen announcing she won't seek reelection.
item.dc:creator: Steve Peoples, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump halts doubling of tariffs on Canadian metals after Ontario suspends electricity price hikes
item.description: The White House trade adviser Peter Navarro said the U.S. president pulled back on his doubling of steel and aluminum tariffs, even as the federal government still plans to place a 25 percent tariff on all steel and aluminum imports starting Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota House is tied after DFL wins special election. Here’s what changes
item.description: DFLer David Gottfried won the suburban Ramsey County seat and is expected to be sworn in to the House of Representatives next week.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: U of M says it's committed to combating antisemitism amid federal probes
item.description: The University of Minnesota says it’s working to address antisemitism on campus, amid federal investigations spurred by protests against Israel. Recent federal actions are raising alarms for pro-Palestinian and civil rights advocates, who say it’s a violation of free speech.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: House GOP passes spending bill, sending plan to avert government shutdown to Senate
item.description: The bill puts significant pressure on Democrats in the Senate, whose votes will be needed in order to avoid a shutdown. The federal government runs out of money at the end of the day on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Deirdre Walsh and Claudia Grisales, MPR News

item.title: Virginia’s Earle-Sears drops first ad confronting Dem opponent over migrant crime, border wall opposition
item.description: An ad from Virginia Lt. Gov. Winsome Earle-Sears plays clips of former Rep. Abigail Spanberger's comments, including her opposition to Trump's border wall, and news clippings.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump floats labeling violence against Tesla dealerships as domestic terrorism
item.description: President Trump hinted he would be open to labeling individuals who carry out violence at Tesla dealerships as “domestic terrorists” following a string of violent demonstrations at the electric vehicle company’s showrooms across the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Department of Education lays off nearly half of workforce
item.description: The Department of Education announced Tuesday it was firing nearly half of its workforce, the latest Trump administration move to shrink the federal government that could face swift legal challenge.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump doubles down on tariffs threat amid market downturn
item.description: President Trump is doubling down on his widespread tariffs threats despite a stock market downturn that has helped fuel anxiety the nation could slide into a recession.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canada suspends 25% electricity tariffs on US
item.description: This comes after what Ford said was a "productive" conversation with U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: National Park Service faces 30% payroll reduction
item.description: The reduction would not necessarily lead to a 30% cut in staffing, since some employees make more than others.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: US hasn't determined who was behind cyberattack that caused outage on Musk's X
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — U.S. officials have not determined who was behind an apparent cyberattack on the social media site X that limited access to the platform for thousands of users, according to a Trump administration official familiar with the ongoing investigation into the matter.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump to increase steel and aluminum tariffs on Canada to 50 percent in response to electricity surcharge
item.description: The uncertainty around Trump's tariff plans have led to a downturn in the stock market, and the president has not ruled out the possibility of a recession this year.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Kentucky bourbon makers fear becoming 'collateral damage' in Trump's trade war
item.description: The iconic American spirit's widening global appeal is caught in the crossfire of trade conflicts instigated by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and DYLAN LOVAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Florida seeks easier voting access for hurricane-displaced citizens
item.description: Last year's hurricanes displaced thousands of Floridians, turning their lives upside down and making it even harder for some to vote during a crucial election year. Now, a Pinellas County state lawmaker wants to make it easier for people to cast their ballots after a storm.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Frontlines with Robert Sherman: Aboard Air Force One for NewsNation
item.description: Never in my wildest dreams did I think I would fly on Air Force One with the president of the United States.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Former US solicitor general to submit written arguments in Eric Adams case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A former U.S. solicitor general who was brought in to help a federal judge decide whether to accept a Justice Department request to drop corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams is set to submit written arguments Friday. Judge Dale E.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Hillsborough commission votes against asking for state DOGE audit
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) — The newly formed Florida State Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) task force is set to use technology and AI to help look for government waste. It will audit colleges and universities, state agencies, along with local governments.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC
